_comment: SHA256 lock for every native binary the installer ships. fetch_binaries.py refuses to proceed on a mismatch. Run scripts/fetch_binaries.py --update once after upstream releases bump versions to repopulate this file.

_ffmpeg_moved_to_pypi._reason: imageio-ffmpeg ships pre-built ffmpeg binaries for Linux, macOS Intel, macOS arm64, and Windows in a single wheel. Removes per-OS lockfile maintenance and avoids the URL-rot risk we hit on first --update (evermeet.cx and gyan.dev both timed out). Access via pinsilico.io.media.ffmpeg_exe().

linux-x86_64._reason: fpocket has no working build pipeline in CI yet (v4.1.3 bundled qhull tree has broken include paths). Install manually: apt install libqhull-dev libnetcdf-dev && git clone + make. Set FPOCKET_BIN env var if installing in a non-PATH location.

macos-arm64._reason: No Homebrew formula and the upstream source build fails in CI. Install manually with brew install qhull netcdf + clone fpocket repo + sed-patch + make. Or use the bundled example-kit pocket for demos.

windows-x86_64._reason: fpocket Makefile is POSIX-only; Windows users skip pocket detection entirely.

linux-x86_64._reason: Upstream openbabel-3-1-1 GitHub tag is 404 (asset renamed). Phase 6 ligand-prep work moves obabel to the openbabel-wheel PyPI package (cross-platform); the SminaVinaAdapter._prepare_ligand_pdbqt placeholder lands the real implementation there. Re-add a lockfile entry only if we need the standalone binary for non-Python callers.

windows-x86_64._reason: smina.exe SourceForge URL 404s as of v1.0.0 build. Smina is a Vina fork; Windows users get AutoDock Vina (vina/windows-x86_64 is populated). If Smina-specific scoring is needed on Windows, build from source or bundle a community-built .exe in a follow-up release.

macos-arm64._reason: AutoDock Vina v1.2.5 has no native macOS arm64 binary in its GitHub release, and the source build needs Boost <= 1.84 (Homebrew only ships 1.85+ which removed boost/filesystem/convenience.hpp). macOS users get docking via smina, a Vina fork with the same scoring function and a drop-in-compatible CLI; the SminaVinaAdapter (sidecar/pinsilico/docking/smina_vina.py) speaks both engines. Re-add native vina/macos-arm64 in v1.0.1 once we vendor Boost 1.84 in CI or upstream ships an arm64 binary.
